what: Rasch Measurement Theory Analysis for R
title: Rasch Measurement Theory Analysis for R
text: easyRasch2 CRAN-targeted R package provides functions Rasch measurement theory analysis workflows. successor easyRasch, offering lightweight, CRAN-ready structure proper namespacing minimal dependencies. Key functions implemented produce simulation-based critical values (cutoffs) conditional item infit MSQ Yen’s Q3 residuals evaluate local dependencies. materials Rasch analysis, see vignette package easyRasch.

title: Rasch Measurement Theory Analysis for R
text: Estimation: Conditional Maximum Likelihood (CML) via eRm, psychotools, iarm item parameters; Weighted Likelihood Estimation (WLE) person parameters; mirt (MML) CML alternatives exist (e.g., Q3 residual correlations). Output: knitr::kable() tables (Quarto-friendly) ggplot2 figures, "dataframe" output options processing. Naming: Functions use RM prefix (e.g., RMlocdepQ3()).

title: Rasch Measurement Theory Analysis for R
text: Install development version GitHub:
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("pgmj/easyRasch2")

title: Rasch Measurement Theory Analysis for R
code: library(easyRasch2)  # Simulate binary item response data set.seed(42) d <- as.data.frame(   matrix(sample(0:1, 200 * 10, replace = TRUE), nrow = 200, ncol = 10) ) colnames(d) <- paste0("Item", 1:10)  # Derive a simulation-based Q3 cutoff (500 iterations by default) cutoff_res <- RMlocdepQ3cutoff(d, iterations = 100, parallel = FALSE, seed = 42)  # Q3 table with simulation-based dynamic cut-off RMlocdepQ3(d, cutoff = cutoff_res$suggested_cutoff)  # Derive a simulation-based conditional item MSQ infit cutoff simfit <- RMinfitcutoff(d, n_cores = 4)  # Infit table with dynamic cut-off RMiteminfit(d, simfit)  # Infit plot with distribution of simulated MSQ values together with observed values RMinfitcutoffPlot(simfit, d)  # Item-restscore using Goodman-Kruskal's gamma RMitemrestscore(d)

Relative Measurement Uncertainty (RMU) — RMUreliability
text: Bayesian-style reliability estimate (Bignardi, Kievit & Bürkner, 2025) computed matrix posterior plausible-value draws. columns input_draws split random two halves; reliability Pearson correlation across persons paired columns two halves, summarised across pairs posterior mean HDCI.

title: Relative Measurement Uncertainty (RMU) — RMUreliability
code: RMUreliability(input_draws, level = 0.95, verbose = FALSE)

title: Relative Measurement Uncertainty (RMU) — RMUreliability
text: input_draws Numeric matrix data.frame draws. Rows subjects; columns draws. Must least two columns; ideally many. level Numeric (0, 1). Width HDCI returned. Default 0.95. verbose Logical. Print summary information input. Default FALSE.

title: Relative Measurement Uncertainty (RMU) — RMUreliability
text: 1-row data.frame columns rmu_estimate, hdci_lowerbound, hdci_upperbound, plus .width/.point/.interval metadata columns added ggdist::mean_hdci().

title: Relative Measurement Uncertainty (RMU) — RMUreliability

title: Bootstrap Item-Restscore Misfit Detection — RMbootRestscore
text: Non-parametric bootstrap item-restscore fit using iarm::item_restscore(). iteration, sample size samplesize drawn data replacement, appropriate Rasch model refitted, item-restscore results classified "overfit", "underfit", "misfit" based BH-adjusted p-value (< .05) sign expected - observed. function returns percentage iterations item flagged.

title: Bootstrap Item-Restscore Misfit Detection — RMbootRestscore
code: RMbootRestscore(   data,   iterations = 200,   samplesize = 600,   parallel = TRUE,   n_cores = NULL,   cutoff = 5,   verbose = FALSE,   seed = NULL,   output = "kable" )

title: Bootstrap Item-Restscore Misfit Detection — RMbootRestscore
text: data data.frame matrix item responses. Items must scored starting 0 (non-negative integers). iterations Integer. Number bootstrap samples (default 200). samplesize Integer. Size bootstrap sample (default 600). Must exceed nrow(data). parallel Logical. Use parallel processing via mirai available (default TRUE). n_cores Integer NULL. Number parallel workers. NULL, getOption("mc.cores") checked first. neither set parallel = TRUE, warning issued execution falls back sequential processing. cutoff Numeric. Items flagged fewer percentage iterations excluded kable output (default 5). effect output = "dataframe" output = "raw". verbose Logical. Show progress bar (default FALSE). seed Integer NULL. Random seed reproducibility. output Either "kable" (default) formatted knitr::kable() table, "dataframe" per-item summary data.frame, "raw" per-iteration long data.frame (useful custom plotting).

title: Bootstrap Item-Restscore Misfit Detection — RMbootRestscore
text: output = "kable": knitr_kable object listing items flagged cutoff% iterations, columns Item, Item-restscore result, % iterations, Conditional MSQ infit, Relative item location, caption noting iteration count, bootstrap size, number complete cases. output = "dataframe": data.frame one row per item × classification combination (Item, item_restscore, n, percent, Infit_MSQ, Relative_location), including "misfit" rows. output = "raw": long data.frame one row per item × successful iteration (iteration, Item, item_restscore, diff, diff_abs), diff = expected - observed.

title: Bootstrap Item-Restscore Misfit Detection — RMbootRestscore
text: Useful large samples, asymptotic test underlying RMitemrestscore can flag items practically misfitting; bootstrapping gives nuanced view probability item actually misfit. dichotomous data (maximum score = 1), full-sample model fitted via eRm::RM() bootstrap iterations refit via eRm::RM() se = FALSE speed. polytomous data (maximum score > 1), full-sample model fitted via eRm::PCM() (item locations can obtained eRm::thresholds()) bootstrap iterations refit via psychotools::pcmodel(..., hessian = FALSE) speed. iarm::item_restscore() accepts model classes. Conditional infit MSQ (computed full sample via iarm::out_infit()) relative item locations reported alongside bootstrap percentages context. Iterations fail (e.g., due convergence issues degenerate bootstrap sample) silently discarded; caption / actual_iterations reflects successful runs. Parallel processing provided mirai package (optional). Install install.packages("mirai") enable parallelisation. iarm package must installed (Suggests, Imports).

title: Bootstrap Item-Restscore Misfit Detection — RMbootRestscore
text: Kreiner, S. (2011). Note Item-Restscore Association Rasch Models. Applied Psychological Measurement, 35(7), 557-561. doi:10.1177/0146621611410227

title: Bootstrap Item-Restscore Misfit Detection — RMbootRestscore
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(42) sim_data <- as.data.frame(   matrix(sample(0:1, 800 * 8, replace = TRUE), nrow = 800, ncol = 8) ) colnames(sim_data) <- paste0("Item", 1:8)  # Default kable output (only items flagged > cutoff%) RMbootRestscore(sim_data, iterations = 100, samplesize = 400,                 parallel = FALSE, seed = 1)  # Per-item summary data.frame (all classifications, including "no misfit") summary_df <- RMbootRestscore(sim_data, iterations = 100, samplesize = 400,                               parallel = FALSE, seed = 1,                               output = "dataframe")  # Per-iteration long data for custom plotting raw_df <- RMbootRestscore(sim_data, iterations = 100, samplesize = 400,                           parallel = FALSE, seed = 1, output = "raw")  # Distribution of (expected - observed) across iterations, per item ggplot(raw_df, aes(x = Item, y = diff)) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +   geom_violin(fill = "grey90", colour = NA) +   geom_jitter(aes(colour = item_restscore),               width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5, size = 0.8) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("overfit"   = "#377eb8",                                  "underfit"  = "#e41a1c",                                  "no misfit" = "grey60")) +   labs(y = "Expected - observed restscore correlation",        colour = NULL) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) } # }

what: DIF analysis via Andersen's likelihood-ratio test — RMdifLR
title: DIF analysis via Andersen's likelihood-ratio test — RMdifLR
text: Splits Rasch model external grouping variable using eRm::LRtest() reports per-group item locations (per-group threshold locations) together standard errors. single function replaces four legacy helpers (RIdifTableLR, RIdifThreshTblLR, RIdifFigureLR, RIdifThreshFigLR) exposing two underlying axes — level (item threshold) output (data.frame, kable, ggplot) — arguments. data preparation pipeline feeds six combinations.

title: DIF analysis via Andersen's likelihood-ratio test — RMdifLR
code: RMdifLR(   data,   dif_var,   model = c("auto", "PCM", "RM"),   level = c("item", "threshold"),   output = c("ggplot", "kable", "dataframe"),   cutoff = 0.5,   conf = 0.95,   sort = FALSE )

title: DIF analysis via Andersen's likelihood-ratio test — RMdifLR
text: data data.frame matrix item responses (non-negative integers, 0-based). One column per item, one row per person. Person IDs grouping variables must included — pass grouping variable separately via dif_var. dif_var Vector length nrow(data) (factor, character, numeric) defining DIF grouping variable. Coerced factor; unused levels dropped. Rows dif_var NA dropped message. Must result least 2 groups cleaning. model One "auto" (default), "PCM", "RM". "auto" fits eRm::RM() data dichotomous (max response = 1) eRm::PCM() otherwise. "RM" errors polytomous data. level One "item" (default) "threshold". "item" reports item's mean threshold location per group; "threshold" reports individual threshold per group. dichotomous (RM) data two views equivalent (one threshold per item). output One "ggplot" (default), "kable", "dataframe". data.frame view always carries Flagged logical column; kable view bolds flagged rows; ggplot view shows confidence intervals. cutoff Numeric NULL. Threshold (logits) Flagged column: row flagged MaxDiff > cutoff, MaxDiff difference largest smallest per-group location item (threshold). Set NULL suppress flagging. Default 0.5. conf Numeric (0, 1). Confidence level used ggplot error bars. Default 0.95. sort Logical. kable output : sort rows MaxDiff (descending). Default FALSE.

title: DIF analysis via Andersen's likelihood-ratio test — RMdifLR
text: data.frame, knitr_kable object, ggplot object, depending output. data.frame one row per item (level = "item") per item × threshold (level = "threshold"), columns Item (Threshold threshold level), one numeric column per group level, column unsplit fit, MaxDiff, Flagged (cutoff non-NULL), matching SE_* columns. Andersen LR test result attached attr(result, "lr_test") data.frame, kable footnote, ggplot caption (LR \(\chi^2\), df, p-value).

title: DIF analysis via Andersen's likelihood-ratio test — RMdifLR

title: Conditional Item Infit MSQ — RMiteminfit
text: Infit MSQ weighted fit statistic emphasises deviations near item location. Values close 1.0 indicate good fit. Values substantially 1.0 suggest underfit (unexpected responses), values substantially 1.0 suggest overfit (overly predictable responses). definition "substantially" depends several factors sample size, needs determined simulation using RMinfitcutoff. general rule--thumb value correct. Conditional infit MSQ statistics computed via iarm::out_infit(), uses conditional distribution sufficient statistics (Müller, 2020). complete cases (rows without NA) used conditional fit calculation. dichotomous data (maximum score = 1), Rasch model fitted via eRm::RM(). Item locations negative beta parameters. Person locations estimated via eRm::person.parameter(). polytomous data (maximum score > 1), Partial Credit Model fitted via eRm::PCM(). Item average locations taken "Location" column threshold parameter table returned eRm::thresholds(); column absent, row means threshold columns used instead. Person locations estimated via eRm::person.parameter(). Relative item location defined item's average location minus sample mean person location, providing measure item targeting. iarm package must installed (Suggests, Imports).

title: Martin-Löf Test of Unidimensionality — RMmartinLof
text: data data.frame matrix item responses (0-based, non-negative integers). Rows NA dropped. partition hypothesised partition items subscales. One : list column-name column-index vectors, e.g. list(c("I1","I2","I3"), c("I4","I5","I6")); vector length ncol(data) indicating item's subscale (factor, character, integer), e.g. c(1,1,1,2,2,2). subscale must contain least two items. Subscales must overlap; items assigned subscale dropped warning. iterations Integer. Maximum number Monte Carlo iterations (default 1000). stopping Character. "none" (default) runs iterations. "sequential" uses Besag & Clifford's (1991) sequential rule: stop soon h simulated statistics exceeded observed value. sequential strategy substantially reduces compute time H0 holds parallelised. h Integer. Persons whose subscore equals minimum maximum subscale produce non-finite WLEs (Inf / -Inf) excluded wle_correlation pairwise. wle_correlation data.frame pairwise Pearson correlations subscale WLEs, columns subscale_a, subscale_b, r, ci_lower, ci_upper (95% CI stats::cor.test), p_value, n (number persons finite WLEs subscales). One row per pair; D = 2, single row. Useful effect-size companion p_value — rejected test r near 1 indicates small effect; r clearly 1 indicates substantive multidimensionality.

title: Martin-Löf Test of Unidimensionality — RMmartinLof
text: routine screening tool. test requires priori partition items subscales; using post-hoc , e.g., partition suggested RMresidualPCA()'s PC1 sign inflate Type-error rate. source papers state explicitly. Test statistic. items partitioned D subscales, total score \(t\) subscores \((t_1, \ldots, t_D)\) (Christensen et al. 2002, eq. 22): $$T = 2\Bigl[\sum_{t_1, \ldots, t_D}   n_{t_1, \ldots, t_D}\log(n_{t_1, \ldots, t_D}/N)   - \sum_t n_t\log(n_t/N)   - \ell_C(\hat{\epsilon}) + \sum_d \ell_C(\hat{\epsilon}^{(d)})\Bigr]$$ \(\ell_C\) conditional log-likelihood \(\hat{\epsilon}^{(d)}\) CML estimates d-th subscale alone. CML fits use psychotools::raschmodel() (RM) psychotools::pcmodel() (PCM) speed. Monte Carlo sampling H0. Following Christensen & Kreiner (2007): () sample N total scores empirical score distribution \(n_t/N\); (b) sampled score, sample item-response vector conditional distribution \(p(x \mid t, \hat{\epsilon})\) given eq. 4 paper. dichotomous data fast algorithm Christensen & Kreiner (2007, p. 23) used (sample without replacement weighted item easinesses). polytomous data recursive \(\gamma\)-function approach used, item's response sampled conditional remaining items' joint score distribution (computed via psychotools::elementary_symmetric_functions()). Iterations fail (e.g., simulated dataset empty category item) silently dropped. Item parameters estimated observed data held fixed across MC iterations. Christensen & Kreiner (2007) use extended likelihood function (Tjur, 1982) empirical score distribution non-parametric estimate latent distribution, distributional assumption \(\theta\) needed.

Psychometrika, 67(4), 563-574. doi:10.1007/BF02295132 Christensen, K. B., & Kreiner, S. (2007). Monte Carlo approach unidimensionality testing polytomous Rasch models. Applied Psychological Measurement, 31(1), 20-30. doi:10.1177/0146621605286204 Besag, J., & Clifford, P. (1991). Sequential Monte Carlo p-values. Biometrika, 78(2), 301-304.

title: Martin-Löf Test of Unidimensionality — RMmartinLof
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) # Build 2-dimensional polytomous data: 4 items per subscale, 5 categories n     <- 400 theta1 <- rnorm(n) theta2 <- 0.6 * theta1 + sqrt(1 - 0.6^2) * rnorm(n) make_pcm <- function(theta, n_items, taus) {   sapply(seq_len(n_items), function(j) {     # ... toy simulation here     sample(0:4, n, replace = TRUE)   }) } dat <- cbind(make_pcm(theta1, 4, NULL), make_pcm(theta2, 4, NULL)) colnames(dat) <- paste0("I", 1:8)  RMmartinLof(dat,             partition = list(c("I1","I2","I3","I4"),                              c("I5","I6","I7","I8")),             iterations = 200, parallel = FALSE, seed = 1)  # Sequential stopping: stop as soon as h = 50 simulated statistics exceed # the observed one (cuts compute time under H0). RMmartinLof(dat,             partition = c(1,1,1,1,2,2,2,2),             iterations = 1000, stopping = "sequential", h = 50,             seed = 1) } # }

text: data data.frame matrix item responses (0-based, non-negative integers). Rows NA dropped. partition format RMmartinLof: list item-name/index vectors, length-ncol(data) vector group labels. subscale must contain least 2 items. output Character. "kable" (default) 2-D pipe-format residual table D = 2 (long-format kable D > 2), "dataframe" underlying long-format data.frame, "ggplot" diverging-fill heatmap (facet_wrap t3 D = 3, error D > 3). flag_threshold Numeric. Cells |residual| > flag_threshold flagged: marked **bold** kable, shown flagged column dataframe. Default 2. color_by Character. output = "ggplot", tile fill colour encodes. "residual" (default) — diverging red-white-blue scale centred 0, directly diagnostic. "n" — sequential blue scale observed cell count, useful spotting whether large-magnitude residuals driven sparse cells. Either way, numeric residual printed inside cell. color_limits Numeric length-2 vector NULL. Caps colour scale output = "ggplot". Default c(-5, 5) color_by = "residual" (sparse-cell residuals (o-e)/sqrt(n*p*(1-p)) can enormous expected counts tiny; capping scale stops outliers compressing rest plot). unclipped residual values still appear cell labels. NULL color_by = "n", uses natural data range. min_expected Numeric NULL. set, cells expected < min_expected residual set NA (appear grey heatmap flagged). Default NULL (filtering). Setting min_expected = 1 (5) analogue Cochran rule sparse-cell chi-square contributions removes residuals whose asymptotic standard normal approximation unreliable.

D > 2, long-format one row per cell. output = "dataframe": long-format data.frame columns t1, ..., tD, total, observed, expected, residual, flagged. output = "ggplot": geom_tile() heatmap (D = 2 3 ).

title: Standardised Residuals from the Joint Subscore Distribution — RMmartinLofResiduals
text: cell joint subscore table (indexed \((t_1, \ldots, t_D)\)), conditional probability H0 given total score \(t = \sum_d t_d\) $$p(t_1, \ldots, t_D \mid t) = \prod_d \gamma^{(d)}_{t_d} / \gamma_t,$$ expected count \(e = n_t \cdot p\), residual \((o - e) / \sqrt{n_t \cdot p \cdot (1 - p)}\). CML estimates unidimensional model used \(\gamma\)-functions. Reading table (D = 2): unidimensional null, residuals patternless roughly N(0, 1). Multidimensionality positively correlated dimensions typically shows positive residuals corners antidiagonal (high t1 + low t2, low t1 + high t2) negative residuals near antidiagonal centre (matched subscores). Negatively correlated dimensions show positive residuals table corners (high/low low/high) negative residuals high/high low/low. See Christensen et al. (2002, §7) worked example. Cells total score observed cases (n_t = 0) uninformative dropped output.

title: Partial Gamma DIF Analysis — RMpartgamDIF
text: Partial gamma (Bjorner et al., 1998) measures association item response exogenous grouping variable, controlling total score. Values near 0 indicate DIF. Recommended interpretive thresholds (Bjorner et al., 1998): negligible DIF: gamma within \([-0.21, 0.21]\), gamma significantly different 0. Slight moderate DIF: gamma within \([-0.31, 0.31]\) (outside \([-0.21, 0.21]\)), significantly outside \([-0.21, 0.21]\). Moderate large DIF: gamma outside \([-0.31, 0.31]\), significantly outside \([-0.21, 0.21]\). iarm package must installed (Suggests, Imports).

title: Simulation-based Cutoff for First-Contrast Eigenvalue — RMpcaCutoff
text: Rule--thumb cutoffs first-contrast eigenvalue depend strongly sample size, test length, item-parameter spread; simulation-based cutoffs tailored data defensible (Chou & Wang, 2010). Per iteration: theta values sampled replacement WLE/MLE estimates derived fitted full-sample model; response data simulated model (psychotools::rrm dichotomous, partial-credit simulator polytomous); model refitted eRm::RM() / eRm::PCM(); standardized residuals extracted via eRm::itemfit()$st.res; prcomp() run; first-contrast eigenvalue recorded. Iterations fail (e.g., due degenerate simulated dataset category represented) silently dropped. iarm package required.

Supports dichotomous data (via eRm::RM() psychotools::rrm()) polytomous data (via eRm::PCM() internal partial credit score simulator). Parallel processing provided mirai package (optional). Install install.packages("mirai") enable parallelisation. iarm package must installed (Suggests, Imports).

what: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability
title: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability
text: Computes three reliability indices Rasch / partial credit model: Person Separation Index (PSI) via eRm::SepRel(), empirical reliability via mirt::empirical_rxx(), Relative Measurement Uncertainty (RMU) via RMUreliability() applied plausible values mirt::fscores().

title: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability
code: RMreliability(   data,   conf_int = 0.95,   draws = 1000,   rmu_iter = 50,   estim = "WLE",   boot = FALSE,   boot_iter = 200,   parallel = TRUE,   n_cores = NULL,   seed = NULL,   verbose = FALSE,   theta_range = c(-10, 10),   output = "kable" )

title: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability
text: data data.frame matrix item responses. Items must scored starting 0 (non-negative integers). conf_int Numeric (0, 1). HDCI width bootstrap CIs RMU. Default 0.95. draws Integer. Number plausible-value draws drawn mirt model RMU calculation. Default 1000. gives stable RMU; computational cost mostly linear. rmu_iter Integer. Number times RMUreliability() repeated set plausible-value draws (repetition uses fresh random column split). Estimates averaged across repetitions stabilise split-induced variability. Default 50. estim Character. Theta estimator used mirt::fscores() empirical reliability plausible-value seed. One "WLE" (default), "EAP", "MAP", "ML". Plausible draws produced Metropolis-Hastings. boot Logical. TRUE, run non-parametric bootstrap obtain CIs PSI Empirical reliability. Default FALSE. boot_iter Integer. Number bootstrap iterations boot = TRUE. Default 200. parallel Logical. Use parallel processing via mirai bootstrap available. Default TRUE. n_cores Integer NULL. Number parallel workers. NULL, getOption("mc.cores") checked first; neither set, bootstrapping falls back sequential. seed Integer NULL. Master random seed reproducibility. verbose Logical. Print progress messages progress bar bootstrap. Default FALSE. theta_range Numeric length-2 vector. Theta limits passed mirt::fscores(). Default c(-10, 10). output Character. "kable" (default) formatted knitr::kable() table, "dataframe" underlying data.frame.

title: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability

title: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability
text: Confidence intervals PSI Empirical reliability obtained non-parametric bootstrap (refitting eRm mirt models resample). RMU interval HDCI correlations across plausible-value draws, averaged rmu_iter random splits draws. CRAN-friendly replacement easyRasch::RIreliability(). TAM-based plausible-values path removed; mirt MML backend. dichotomous data (max = 1), mirt::mirt(..., itemtype = "Rasch") eRm::RM() fitted. polytomous data, itemtype used mirt eRm::PCM() eRm. PSI computed eRm::SepRel() person-parameter object eRm::person.parameter(). Note PSI excludes respondents extreme (min/max) raw scores construction. RMU Bignardi, Kievit, & Bürkner (2025), modified use mirt plausible values rather fully Bayesian posterior draws (see Mislevy, 1991, plausible-values framework). Bootstrap iterations fail converge either mirt eRm silently dropped.

Rows NA dropped PCA, since prcomp() accept missing values. cutoff Optional. list returned RMpcaCutoff (suggested_cutoff used), single numeric value use cutoff directly. provided, result includes Flagged column (logical: eigenvalue simulated bound?) kable caption notes cutoff. n_components Integer. Number eigenvalues report. Capped number items. Default 5. output Character. Access via attr(result, "variance_partition"). output = "loadings": ggplot showing item's PC1 loading x-axis Rasch item location y-axis, dashed reference lines zero, variance partition figure caption. Item names labelled via ggrepel::geom_text_repel() ggrepel installed; otherwise plain geom_text().

title: PCA of Standardized Rasch Residuals — RMresidualPCA
text: Rule--thumb thresholds first-contrast eigenvalue (e.g., "> 2" heuristic occasionally cited Winsteps documentation) reliable indicators multidimensionality; first-contrast eigenvalue correctly fitting unidimensional model varies systematically sample size, test length, item-parameter spread. Empirical (simulated) bounds tailored data structure used instead — see RMpcaCutoff, Chou & Wang (2010) underlying simulation argument. PCA performed standardized residuals returned eRm::itemfit()$st.res, (observed - expected) / sqrt(var) Rasch model. reported eigenvalues unrotated; rotation appropriate interpreting multidimensional solution obscures dominant first contrast dimensionality assessment concerned . Item locations loadings plot computed per-item mean Andrich thresholds polytomous data (PCM) -beta dichotomous data (RM). variance partition follows Linacre's CML/MLE convention: per-item observed variance compared per-item expected variance fitted model, summed across items. Expected scores computed MLE person locations (via eRm::person.parameter()) CML item parameters eRm::RM() / eRm::PCM(). Persons extreme raw scores (finite MLE theta) excluded partition, matching sample used eRm::SepRel() PCA .

title: Person-Item Targeting Plot (Wright Map) — RMtargeting
text: data data.frame matrix item responses. Items must scored starting 0 (non-negative integers). Missing values (NA) allowed. robust Logical. FALSE (default), histogram annotations use mean ± SD. TRUE, median ± MAD used instead. sort_items Character string controlling item ordering y-axis bottom panel. "data" (default) preserves column order data (first item top). "location" sorts items average threshold location (easiest top, hardest bottom). bins Integer. Number bins histograms. Default number unique scores plus one, less 15. xlim Numeric vector length 2. Initial lower upper limits shared x-axis. Automatically expanded person item threshold values fall outside limits. ci_level Numeric. Confidence level item threshold error bars. Default 0.95 (95% CI). Set NULL hide error bars. person_fill Fill colour person histogram. Default "#0072B2" (blue). threshold_fill Fill colour item threshold histogram. Default "#D55E00" (vermillion). height_ratios Numeric vector length 3 specifying relative heights top (person), middle (threshold), bottom (dot-whisker) panels. Default c(3, 2, 5). output Character string. "figure" (default) returns combined patchwork plot. "list" returns named list three ggplot objects (p1, p2, p3) customisation.

title: Person-Item Targeting Plot (Wright Map) — RMtargeting
text: output = "figure": patchwork object (combined ggplot). output = "list": named list elements p1 (person histogram), p2 (threshold histogram), p3 (item threshold dot-whisker plot).

title: Person-Item Targeting Plot (Wright Map) — RMtargeting
text: Together, top middle panels form back--back histogram makes easy assess whether test well-targeted sample. Estimation method selection. function checks whether item response category fewer 3 observations. categories least 3 responses, item threshold locations standard errors estimated via Conditional Maximum Likelihood (CML) using eRm::RM() (dichotomous) eRm::PCM() (polytomous), SEs eRm::thresholds(). category fewer 3 responses, function falls back Marginal Maximum Likelihood (MML) estimation via mirt::mirt() itemtype = "Rasch" SE = TRUE, numerically stable sparse-category conditions. message emitted MML fallback used. cases, item threshold locations centered (shifted grand mean thresholds equals zero). Person estimates obtained via Maximum Likelihood (ML) eRm::person.parameter(), uses spline interpolation extrapolate location estimates persons extreme scores (-zero perfect). Persons spline interpolation fails receive NA excluded histogram. Confidence intervals item thresholds based Wald-type intervals: threshold estimate ± z × SE, z standard normal quantile corresponding ci_level. ggplot2 patchwork packages must installed (Suggests, Imports).

title: Person-Item Targeting Plot (Wright Map) — RMtargeting

title: Tile Plot of Item Response Distributions — RMtileplot
text: Creates tile (heat map) plot showing distribution responses across items response categories. cell displays count (percentage) responses, optional conditional highlighting cells low counts. Optional faceting grouping variable provided inspecting subgroup response distributions DIF analyses — particularly useful spotting empty categories -represented subgroups fitting Rasch models per group.

title: Tile Plot of Item Response Distributions — RMtileplot
text: data data.frame wide format containing item response columns. column one item, row one person. columns must numeric (integer-valued). Response categories may coded starting 0 1. include person IDs, grouping variables, non-item columns — supply grouping variable separately via group. group Optional vector length nrow(data) (factor, character, numeric) defining grouping variable. provided, plot faceted group counts / percentages computed within group. Default NULL (faceting). Persons NA group excluded. cutoff Integer. Cells counts value highlighted (highlight = TRUE). Default 10. highlight Logical. TRUE (default), cell labels counts cutoff displayed red. includes empty cells (n = 0), useful identifying gaps response distribution. percent Logical. TRUE, cell labels show percentages instead raw counts. Percentages computed within item (within group, group supplied). Default FALSE. text_color Character. Colour non-highlighted cell labels. Default "orange". item_labels Optional character vector descriptive labels items (y-axis), length ncol(data). Default NULL uses column names. category_labels Optional character vector labels response categories (x-axis), length number categories spanning min max observed value. Default NULL uses numeric category values. group_labels Optional character vector length nlevels(.factor(group)) override displayed facet labels. Order corresponds levels(.factor(group)). facet_ncol Integer NULL. Number columns facet grid group supplied. Default NULL (ggplot2 chooses). output Character. "ggplot" (default) returns plot; "dataframe" returns underlying per-cell counts (one row per item × category, plus group supplied).

title: Tile Plot of Item Response Distributions — RMtileplot

title: easyRasch2 0.5.7
text: New RMdifLR() testing DIF categorical variables using Andersens’s Likelihood Ratio test implemented package eRm Optional group faceting, useful DIF-analyses. Also added post-processing function RMmartinLofresiduals().

title: easyRasch2 0.5.6
text: Added RMresidualPCA() evaluating patterns standardized residuals RM/PCM. Outputs either table eigenvalues explained variance figure standardized loadings first residual contrast item locations. Added RMpcaCutoff() determine simulation-based critical value largest eigenvalue.

title: easyRasch2 0.5.5
text: Added RMbootRestscore() use large sample sizes. See https://pgmj.github.io/rasch_itemfit/ details. Added RMscoreSE() produces transformation table (figure) ordinal sum scores WLE (Weighted Likelihood Estimation) interval scores. Modified RMlocdepQ3cutoff() improve speed.

title: easyRasch2 0.5.4
text: RMtargeting() Wright map style plots. RMinfitcutoff_mi() uses mids object containing multiple imputated datasets (output mice::mice()) run simulations datasets combines results. Splits iterations across imputations (e.g. 250 total / 5 imputations = 50 ). RMiteminfit_mi() calculates pools conditional infit imputated datasets optionally uses RMinfitcutoff_mi() output cutoff values. Works existing RMinfitcutoffPlot()

title: easyRasch2 0.5.3
text: Plot function RMpgLDplot(), similar RMpgDIFplot() NOTE: simulation-based cutoffs yet evaluated systematic way. Default choices may sensible, please try different numbers iterations hdci_width.

title: easyRasch2 0.5.2
text: Also plot function RMpgDIFplot(), similar RMinfitcutoffPlot() NOTE: simulation-based cutoffs yet evaluated systematic way. Default choices may sensible, please try different numbers iterations hdci_width.

title: easyRasch2 0.5.1
text: New function RMinfitcutoffPlot() illustrate distribution simulated conditional item infit MSQ values together observed value.

title: easyRasch2 0.5.0
text: RMinfitcutoff() gains cutoff_method hdci_width parameters: default (cutoff_method = "hdci", hdci_width = 0.999), per-item cutoff intervals now computed using Highest Density Interval via ggdist::hdci() (99.9% HDCI). Set cutoff_method = "quantile" restore previous behaviour (2.5th/97.5th percentiles). ggdist package required cutoff_method = "hdci" (added Suggests). returned list now also includes cutoff_method hdci_width fields. RMiteminfit() caption updated: cutoff return value RMinfitcutoff(), kable caption now states cutoff method, e.g. "Cutoff values based 250 simulation iterations (99.9% HDCI)." "Cutoff values based 250 simulation iterations (2.5th/97.5th percentile).".

title: easyRasch2 0.4.0
text: RMiteminfit() gains optional cutoff parameter: Accepts return value RMinfitcutoff() ($item_cutoffs data.frame directly). provided, per-item cutoff boundaries (Infit_low, Infit_high) logical Flagged column added "dataframe" "kable" output. kable caption includes number simulation iterations available. New RMinfitcutoff(): Simulation-based (parametric bootstrap) cutoff determination [RMiteminfit()]. Supports dichotomous polytomous data. Optional parallel processing via mirai (falls back sequential installed). Returns per-item 2.5th 97.5th percentiles simulated infit outfit MSQ distributions ($item_cutoffs), together full iteration-level results ($results). Requires iarm package (Suggests).

title: easyRasch2 0.3.0
text: New RMiteminfit(): Computes conditional infit MSQ statistics item via iarm::out_infit(), enriched item locations relative sample mean person location. Supports dichotomous (Rasch model via eRm::RM()) polytomous (Partial Credit Model via eRm::PCM()) data. Requires iarm package (Suggests). Output options: "kable" (default, plain-text knitr::kable()) "dataframe". Optional sort = "infit" sorts infit MSQ descending. complete cases used conditional fit calculation. New RMitemrestscore(): Computes observed model-expected item-restscore correlations via iarm::item_restscore(), enriched absolute differences observed expected values, item average locations, item locations relative sample mean person location. Supports dichotomous (Rasch model via eRm::RM()) polytomous (Partial Credit Model via eRm::PCM()) data. Requires iarm package (Suggests). Output options: "kable" (default, plain-text knitr::kable()) "dataframe". Optional sort = "diff" sorts absolute difference descending.

title: easyRasch2 0.2.0
text: RMlocdepQ3(): cutoff now optional (default NULL). omitted, raw Q3 residual correlation matrix returned without dynamic cut-applied. provided, dynamic cut-(mean Q3 + cutoff) shown kable caption . Supersedes requirement always supply cutoff value (PRs #2 #3). New RMlocdepQ3cutoff(): Simulation-based (parametric bootstrap) cutoff determination RMlocdepQ3(). Supports dichotomous polytomous data. Optional parallel processing via mirai (falls back sequential installed). $suggested_cutoff 99th percentile simulated Q3 max–mean distribution. New internal helpers R/utils-simulation.R: sim_poly_item(), sim_partial_score(), extract_item_thresholds(). Dependencies: eRm, psychotools (>= 0.7-3), parallel, utils moved/added Imports. mirai added Suggests.

title: easyRasch2 0.1.0
text: Initial package structure. Added RMlocdepQ3() Yen’s Q3 residual correlation analysis local dependence.
